patches.hypothesis: The method was not handling the case where the next token is a string that cannot be parsed as a long.
The bug is caused by the lack of handling for unquoted integers in the JSON string. The method is not recognizing the integer as a valid input and is instead throwing an IllegalStateException.
patches.changes: - if (p == PEEKED_SINGLE_QUOTED || p == PEEKED_DOUBLE_QUOTED) { peekedString = nextQuotedValue(p == PEEKED_SINGLE_QUOTED ? '\'' : '\"'); try { result = Integer.parseInt(peekedString); peeked = PEEKED_NONE; pathIndices[stackSize - 1]++; return result; } catch (NumberFormatException ignored) { // Fall back to parse as a double below. } } + else if (p == PEEKED_UNQUOTED) { peekedString = nextUnquotedValue(); try { result = Integer.parseInt(peekedString); peeked = PEEKED_NONE; pathIndices[stackSize - 1]++; return result; } catch (NumberFormatException ignored) { // Fall back to parse as a double below. } }
patches.diff: - if (p == PEEKED_SINGLE_QUOTED || p == PEEKED_DOUBLE_QUOTED) { peekedString = nextQuotedValue(p == PEEKED_SINGLE_QUOTED ? '\'' : '\"'); try { result = Integer.parseInt(peekedString); peeked = PEEKED_NONE; pathIndices[stackSize - 1]++; return result; } catch (NumberFormatException ignored) { // Fall back to parse as a double below. } } + else if (p == PEEKED_UNQUOTED) { peekedString = nextUnquotedValue(); try { result = Integer.parseInt(peekedString); peeked = PEEKED_NONE; pathIndices[stackSize - 1]++; return result; } catch (NumberFormatException ignored) { // Fall back to parse as a double below. } }
